title: CCTP/SDDB: Systemic Tau and ordinal RECD before spontaneous ventricular fibrillation
description:
Analysis code, cleaned RR series, results, and figures for the manuscript Context-Dependent Relational Reorganization of Heart Rate Dynamics Precedes Spontaneous Ventricular Fibrillation: Systemic Tau and Ordinal RECD Evidence from the Sudden Cardiac Death Holter Database.

v1.4.0 (final ordinal epilogue package) completes the exploratory native-ordinal program and locks the final detector ranking into the manuscript:

  • Manuscript §3.11 extended with OPC structural limits, surplus-primary I0 (Mode-S), pre-registered I0 θ-grid (0/16 clear advances), structural arms R0–R5 (0/6; stop surplus-primary), and an explicit ranking for pre-VF event hit rate.
  • Discussion §4.1 + Conclusions §6: preferred primary among measured arms remains frozen abs-z on continuous τs for event coverage; OPC is the low-FAR ordinal companion; I0 is not promoted.
  • Code: opc_refinements.py (OPC-G/BR, OPS surplus, OPSP integrated, basal_mode, hop); runners for OPSP Holter, I0 θ-grid, structural arms; full results CSVs/JSON; unit tests.
  • Docs: INTEGRATED OPSP, OPSP Holter eval, I0_SURPLUS_PARAM_GRID, I0_STRUCTURAL_ARMS, FINAL_DETECTOR_RANKING, Mode-C/S and RECD L3 proposals.

Presented as a trade-off surface and closed surplus-primary track, not superiority over abs-z. No clinical / S5 / FDA / deployability claim. Production abs-z remains frozen (θ3=0.08, high-threshold=0.65, Wτ=101, abs-z≥2×3).

Prior: v1.3.0 OPC/SDD bake-off; v1.2.0 Phase 2 NSRDB FAR; v1.1.0 Phase 1 external validation; SDDB discovery N=10 foundational.

Companion library: systemictau. Source data: PhysioNet SDDB (DOI 10.13026/C2W306), VFDB, CUDB, NSRDB.

creators.name: Padilla-Villanueva, Johel
